class.name: NeighbourhoodServer
class.brief_description: Node that provides fast spatial neighbourhood queries over subscribed [Node2D] instances.
class.description: [NeighbourhoodServer] maintains a uniform spatial grid that is rebuilt at a configurable interval during physics processing. [Node2D] instances register themselves via [method subscribe] and can be queried by world position using [method get_next] and [method get_all]. Subscribers outside the [member domain] rectangle are ignored and not placed in the grid. The domain can be visualised in the editor and at runtime via [member draw_domain].

method.name: subscribe
method.description: Registers [param node] with a collision [param layer] bitmask and an arbitrary [param data] payload. If [param node] is already subscribed its entry is overwritten.

method.name: unsubscribe
method.description: Removes [param node] from the server so it is no longer returned by spatial queries. Safe to omit if the node is about to be freed — stale entries are cleaned up automatically on the next refresh.

method.name: get_next
method.description: Returns the data payload of the nearest subscriber to [param position] whose layer matches [param layer_mask]. Returns [code]null[/code] if no match is found. [param max_distance] limits the search radius in world units. Use [code]0.0[/code] for unlimited range.

method.name: get_all
method.description: Returns an [Array] of data payloads for all subscribers within [param max_distance] of [param position] whose layer matches [param layer_mask].

members.member: Width and height of each spatial grid cell in world units. Smaller values improve query precision at the cost of more cells to manage; larger values reduce overhead but increase the number of subscribers checked per query. Should be somewhere around the average interaction radius of our queries.
Minimum time in seconds between grid rebuilds. Set to [code]0.0[/code] to rebuild every physics frame. Higher values reduce CPU cost at the expense of query freshness.

When [code]true[/code] (default), subscriber positions are read via [method Node2D.get_global_position]. Set to [code]false[/code] to use [method Node2D.get_position] (local space), which is significantly faster when all subscribers share the same parent and no global transform traversal is needed.
The world-space rectangle that defines the active area of the server. Subscribers located outside this rectangle are ignored during each grid refresh and will not appear in query results.

When [code]true[/code], the server draws the [member domain] boundary and grid overlay using [method CanvasItem.draw_rect] and [method CanvasItem.draw_line]. In debug builds, queried cells are also highlighted in red proportional to their query frequency.

Interval in seconds between redraws of the query-count heatmap. Only has an effect when [member draw_domain] is [code]true[/code] and the project is running in a debug build. Set to a negative value to disable query drawing entirely. [b]Warning:[/b] This feature is intended for debugging only. Frequent redraws at low intervals are very expensive at scale and will noticeably impact performance.

signal.name: debug_info
signal.description: Emitted at various points during processing to expose internal timing and diagnostic data. Only available in debug builds! [param name] may be: [code]main_thread_id[/code], [code]refresh_thread_id[/code], [code]refresh_total_ms[/code], [code]max_querycount[/code].
